misconceptions.title: Did Not Flip the Second Fraction
misconceptions.description: Students mistakenly divide fractions without flipping (taking the reciprocal of) the second fraction. This error results from not fully understanding the operation of division of fractions, assuming it resembles whole number division directly.
successIndicators: Student flips the second fraction before performing division.
Student articulates the concept of reciprocal in fraction division.
Student correctly computes several practice problems involving division of fractions.
Student explains verbally or in writing why flipping the second fraction is necessary.

misconceptions.title: Reversed Order of Division
misconceptions.description: Students incorrectly order the fractions in division operations, treating 'A divided by B' as 'B divided by A'. This reflects a misunderstanding of the order of operations specific to division of fractions.
successIndicators: Student writes division expressions in the correct order.
Student explains the importance of order in division of fractions.
Student consistently solves fractional division problems correctly with proper order.
Student identifies errors when fractions are incorrectly ordered by others.

misconceptions.title: Mistaking Division for Multiplication
misconceptions.description: Students interpret division of fractions as multiplication, possibly due to skipping the flipping step or misremembering the instruction to "invert and multiply" as just "multiply."
successIndicators: Student differentiates between division and multiplication operations clearly.
Student correctly completes division problems after identifying the reciprocal.
Student articulates the difference between division and multiplication processes.
Student consistently uses 'invert and multiply' accurately during practice.

misconceptions.title: Dividing by Numerators Only
misconceptions.description: Students divide fractions by only dividing the numerators and retaining the original denominators, showing a lack of understanding of the requirement to multiply by the reciprocal.
successIndicators: Student utilizes full division process involving both numerators and denominators.
Student solves fractional division and verifies answers against expected results.
Student explains the sequence of operations in division of fractions.
Student identifies and corrects errors when the division process is incomplete.
